# UC-13.7.6 · Fluent Bit Chunk Flush Latency P95 Trending

## Description

Rising flush latency means outputs (Splunk HEC, Kafka, S3) are slowing or network RTT is degrading; p95 catches tail pain that averages hide. Early warning prevents chunk backlog growth on edge agents.

## Value

Stabilizes edge logging on constrained hardware and prevents storm-induced drops.

## Implementation

Normalize nanoseconds to milliseconds once at ingest. If metrics lack per-output latency, approximate with `storage_chunks.up` age fields. Correlate with regional network dashboards.

## SPL

```spl
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentbit:storage" OR sourcetype="fluentbit:metrics"
| eval flush_ms=coalesce(flush_time_ms, flush_duration_ms, if(isnotnull(chunk_flush_time_ns), chunk_flush_time_ns/1000000, null()))
| where isnotnull(flush_ms)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats perc95(flush_ms) as p95_flush, count as n by _time, output_name, host
| eventstats median(p95_flush) as med by output_name
| where p95_flush > med * 2 AND n > 20
| sort -p95_flush
```

## Visualization

Line chart (p95 by output), heatmap (host × output), single-value (worst p95).

## References

- [Fluent Bit — Storage](https://docs.fluentbit.io/manual/administration/buffering-and-storage)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
